## [00:00 - 03:00]

So this is a case study with Deirdre Fay and she did a joint venture lunch. So I want to walk you through that. She had some great learning. She did a great job with this. So Deirdre is a therapist. She's been in private practice for 25 years. She specializes in healing PTSD and she's written four books. Her health is all about, well, it's about personal transformation. She does that through courses, audios, books, and transforming suffering into compassion. So the program, her course is called Living Untriggered, Healing Trauma with Meditation Skills. So this is all about easy meditation that help with everyday life, with the problems you've got and even with trauma. So she first did a seed launch to create this course. She built the program over four weeks plus she did a bonus webinar. So she went classic PLF style. She had a list of 2,500. She made 69 sales for a total revenue of $18,214. Her price point was $269. So that was the seed launch. And she came back and did a joint venture launch. She had nine joint venture partners. Five of those partners made sales. Number one had 29 sales. Number two had four sales. Number three and four had three sales each. And number five had five sales. So nine partners, five made sales. Four didn't make sales. And so if we look at this, it was 33, 36, 39, 40. If my math is right, it was 40 sales. 29 of those sales divided by 40. So 72% of the sales came from one partner. So this is something I talk about throughout. Whenever I talk about these joint venture launches, that it's always an 80-20 rule where 80% of your sales come from 20% of your partners. And in reality, it's usually a 90-10 rule. So here, 72.5%. So 73% of the sales came from one out of nine partners. So roughly, let's just say it's a little more than 10% of our partners or a little bit more. And that one partner did a 72% of the sales. So now I throw a lot of numbers out here. But you see, it's plain as day right in front of you that if one partner did 29 sales, you need to focus on your best partners. And when you're first starting out with your first joint venture launch, you don't necessarily know who your best partners are. But once you start to figure it out, your top partners will make a huge difference in your launch results. So focus, this is not every partner's created equal.
